summary: Excellent soundboard‑with‑audience bleed capture of a strong “Playing > Cosmic Charlie” jam from the Orpheum run, highlighted by tight transitions and inspired improvisation.
review: The recording is praised for its clear soundboard source with just enough audience ambience to add atmosphere, delivering a crisp mix that lets every instrument shine. Musically the show shines on the “Playing > Cosmic Charlie” sandwich and the Wheel‑to‑Playin transition, with Phil’s daring 7/4 riff attempts and Jerry’s signature licks adding extra flavor, even if a few reviewers note it falls short of the legendary ‘75 versions.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 4‑5) and enthusiastic comments about both the performance and the sound quality push the rating into the good‑to‑great range; recording quality is described as excellent, while show quality is strong with only minor comparative criticisms.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Great, clear soundboard mix with a subtle audience layer that adds ambience but does not muddy the instruments; no dropout or balance issues reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second‑set jam sandwich (Playin → Cosmic Charlie) and the Wheel‑to‑Playin transition are highlighted as fluid and exciting, though a few reviewers feel an elusive “x‑factor” is missing compared to other nights.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His signature licks weave through the jam; reviewers note he tinkers around the theme with classic ’75‑style phrasing.
band_member_comments.Phil: Attempts a daring 7/4 riff during the “Stronger Than Dirt”‑styled section, which several fans point out as a bold but not fully realized moment.
